name: Request a Transcript from the Registrar
description: Call a college / high school registrar to request an official transcript — sealed vs. unsealed, electronic vs. paper, standard vs. rush — and unblock holds, payment friction, or third-party-recipient quirks that stall the request.
disclaimer: Schools may withhold transcripts for unpaid balances, library fines, or other holds. FERPA governs who can request transcripts on behalf of an adult student — the registrar will require either the student's authorization or proof of legal guardianship for a minor.

context.when_to_use: The user needs an official transcript sent to a third party (grad school, employer, licensing board, NCAA Eligibility Center, another college for transfer credit) and either (a) the online portal isn't producing the right delivery method, (b) there's a hold blocking the order, or (c) the deadline is tight enough that standard 5-10 business day processing won't work.
preconditions: User has Student ID, dates of attendance, and (if changed) the name on the record at time of attendance.
User has the exact recipient name, address, email, and any institutional code (CEEB / SAO / Parchment / Credentials Inc. / National Student Clearinghouse).
User knows whether the recipient REQUIRES a sealed paper transcript or accepts e-transcript (this varies — bar exams and some grad schools still require paper-sealed).
User has a credit card or can pay the fee — most registrars charge $5–$25 per copy plus rush fees.

principles: The registrar's first answer is the policy answer. Discretionary exceptions exist — but you have to ask, and you have to give them a reason.
Rush isn't always available, but expedited delivery (FedEx overnight) almost always is — they're different fees and different services.
Holds block transcripts even if you've paid. The hold has to be lifted in the system. If you paid yesterday, the bursar's update may not have synced — ask the registrar to call the bursar directly.
Sealed means the envelope is unbroken with the registrar's signature/stamp across the flap. If a recipient requires 'sealed', a PDF you forwarded yourself does NOT count.
Many third-party recipients (LSAC, AMCAS, AACOMAS, CSDCAS, NCAA) want the transcript sent to THEM, not to you. Routing through the user defeats the purpose and often gets rejected.

Errors on transcripts (wrong grade, missing course, name change) require a correction request from the original department — the registrar publishes what departments certify. Call the department first.

phrases.opener: Hi, I'd like to order an official transcript. My name is [name], Student ID [ID], I attended from [year] to [year]. Could you walk me through the options for getting it to [recipient] by [date]?
phrases.ask_about_holds: Could you check whether there are any holds on my account that would block a transcript order? I want to clear those before I submit so the order doesn't bounce.
phrases.rush_request: I have a hard deadline of [date]. Is rush processing available, or expedited shipping, or both? What's the realistic total turnaround if I order today?
phrases.sealed_clarification: The recipient is asking for a sealed transcript. Just to confirm — what your office sends is the official sealed copy that they consider unopened, correct? Either by mail or via a secure e-transcript provider?
phrases.third_party_routing: The transcript needs to go directly from your office to [LSAC / AMCAS / NCAA / etc.]. Their school code is [code]. Can you confirm you have that recipient in your system and that the e-transcript will route correctly?
phrases.fee_waiver_ask: I'm a current student / alum applying for [graduate school / a fellowship / a job]. Do you have a fee-waiver process for active applications, or a flat number of free transcripts per year?
phrases.supervisor_request: Thanks for checking — could I speak with the Registrar or an Associate Registrar? I have a deadline issue and I want to make sure I'm not missing any options.
phrases.graceful_close_success: Thank you. To confirm: order placed under reference [number], delivery to [recipient] via [method] by [date], total $[amount] charged to [card]. May I have an email confirmation, and your name in case I need to follow up?

tactics.name: Check for holds before ordering
tactics.script: Use `ask_about_holds`. Common silent holds: unpaid library fines under $20, unreturned lab equipment, parking tickets, missing health-insurance waiver, missing exit-counseling for federal loans. Clearing these first prevents a failed order.

tactics.name: Clarify sealed vs. e-transcript expectation
tactics.script: Use `sealed_clarification`. Some bar associations and a few grad programs still reject e-transcripts. When in doubt, order both — paper for the file, e-transcript for speed.

tactics.name: Use the recipient's institutional code
tactics.when: Sending to LSAC, AMCAS, AACOMAS, CSDCAS, NCAA, Parchment, Credentials Inc., or National Student Clearinghouse.
tactics.script: Use `third_party_routing`. These services have school-specific codes that route the transcript directly into the user's application file. Sending to a generic mailing address means manual matching and weeks of delay.

tactics.name: Ask about rush AND expedited shipping separately
tactics.script: Use `rush_request`. Rush = faster processing inside the registrar's office. Expedited = faster shipping after it leaves. They stack. Some offices also offer 'same-day in-person pickup' that's faster than either.

tactics.name: Ask about fee waivers
tactics.when: User is unemployed, low-income, currently enrolled, applying to grad school via a fee-waiver program (LSAC FAP, AAMC FAP), or has limited copies/year built into tuition.
tactics.script: Use `fee_waiver_ask`. Most schools include 2–5 free transcripts per lifetime or per year; alums sometimes forget this benefit exists.

tactics.name: Escalate to the Associate Registrar for exceptions
tactics.when: Hold can't be lifted same-day, or rush isn't normally offered but deadline is critical.
tactics.script: Use `supervisor_request`. The Registrar and Associate Registrar can override processing-queue rules, hand-deliver to the bursar to clear a hold, or authorize same-day issuance in a way front-desk staff cannot.

tactics.name: Request a 'pending' transcript if final grades aren't in yet
tactics.when: User is a current-term student and needs a transcript before finals post.
tactics.script: Ask whether the registrar offers an 'in-progress' or 'partial' transcript that shows current registration. Some grad-school applications accept these as interim records.

boundaries: Do NOT request a transcript on someone else's behalf unless you have FERPA authorization in writing (or are legal guardian of a minor) — the registrar will refuse and may flag the account.
Do NOT alter or open a sealed transcript. Doing so voids the seal and the recipient will refuse it.
Do NOT promise to pay a hold during the call without operator approval — confirm the dollar amount and the bursar's payment channel first.
Do NOT ask the registrar to fix a grade. That's a faculty / department / academic-standards-committee process; registrar only records what's certified.
Do NOT email an unsealed PDF to a recipient that required sealed and claim it counts — it doesn't, and re-submitting late may miss the deadline.
success_signals: Registrar confirms electronic delivery to the recipient's institutional code.
Email confirmation lands in the user's inbox before the call ends.
Rush + expedited combined delivery is in writing.
failure_signals: Hold can't be cleared until the bursar reopens the next business day — and the deadline is tomorrow.
Registrar says e-transcript isn't supported for this recipient and paper-only via mail won't arrive in time.
Account is in the archive and physical records require 4–6 weeks to retrieve (common for schools that have changed student-information systems).
Name on record doesn't match the user's current legal name and recipient requires matching name — needs a name-change petition first.

exit_strategy.on_success: Confirm order number, method, recipient, fee, ETA. Ask for email confirmation. Save a screenshot of the tracking page if e-transcript.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Get the specific blocker in writing. Decide with the operator: (a) pay the hold same-day and re-order, (b) request a deadline extension from the recipient, (c) escalate to the Registrar, (d) request an in-progress / unofficial transcript as a stopgap.
follow_ups: Email the operator the order number, ETA, and any tracking link.
If recipient requires confirmation of receipt, set a check-in 2 business days after expected delivery.
Document the registrar's email so future transcript orders can skip the phone tree.

required_user_info: Student ID, full legal name, and name-at-attendance if different
Dates of attendance and degree(s) awarded
Recipient name, address/email, institutional code if applicable
Any known holds (library, bursar, parking, etc.)
